project.name: EuroSAT Refine Research
project.description: Research-based learning project for EuroSAT Sentinel-2 land-cover classification. It reproduces and refines supervised baselines across RGB, 13-band, spectral-index, augmentation, optimizer, and low-label experiment conditions using PyTorch/TorchGeo training pipelines and YAML run configs.

layers.name: Experiment Entry Points
layers.description: CLI scripts and notebooks used to launch, verify, and inspect EuroSAT experiments.

layers.name: Data Pipeline
layers.description: Dataset loading, split generation, spectral band handling, statistics, and reproducible split artifacts.

layers.name: Model Architecture
layers.description: Model construction and channel adaptation for RGB, 13-band, and spectral-index ResNet experiments.

layers.name: Training and Evaluation
layers.description: Epoch training, evaluation, mixed augmentations, and optimization behavior.

layers.name: Utilities and Tracking
layers.description: Configuration loading, deterministic seeding, and experiment tracking support.

layers.name: Experiment Configurations
layers.description: YAML recipes for baseline, refinement, optimizer, augmentation, and low-label research runs.

layers.name: Research Documentation
layers.description: Project overview, protocol memory, research notes, result tables, presentation material, and dependencies.

layers.name: Repository Scaffolding
layers.description: Small placeholder or package marker files that preserve repository structure.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and project memory to understand the reproduce-refine-research workflow and current EuroSAT conclusions.

tour.title: Dataset and Splits
tour.description: Inspect how EuroSAT samples, train/validation/test splits, label-fraction subsets, and channel statistics feed the experiments.

tour.title: Bands and Transforms
tour.description: Follow band selection, spectral-index computation, normalization vectors, and augmentation transforms before tensors enter the model.

tour.title: Model Adaptation
tour.description: Review the ResNet-50 construction and first-convolution adaptation that supports RGB and multispectral Sentinel-2 inputs.

tour.title: Training Entry Point
tour.description: Trace the CLI path that loads YAML configs, resolves bands, builds loaders and models, and runs one seed of an experiment.

tour.title: Optimization and Evaluation
tour.description: Inspect the training loop, evaluation pass, mixed augmentations, seeding, and W&B logging around each experiment run.

tour.title: Research Outputs
tour.description: Finish with analysis notes, result tables, and presentation material that summarize the refine phase and frame the low-label research direction.
